echoesReleaseDateAndPlatforms.title: Echoes of Aincrad Release Date, Platforms, and Preload
echoesReleaseDateAndPlatforms.intro: Echoes of Aincrad is scheduled for a July 2026 launch across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Digital PC. Console and Steam release times vary by region, and preload support is different on each platform.

items.label: Official release date
items.detail: Bandai Namco lists July 10, 2026 as the official launch date for Echoes of Aincrad across launch platforms.

items.label: Steam release listing
items.detail: The Steam store page lists Jul 9, 2026 because PC unlock timing falls on July 9 in some regions, including PDT and EDT.

items.label: Supported platforms
items.detail: The game is available at launch on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Digital PC via Steam.

items.label: Nintendo Switch support
items.detail: Echoes of Aincrad is not listed for Nintendo Switch or Switch 2 at launch.

items.label: Console release examples
items.detail: Examples include 00:00 on July 10 in CEST, EDT, and BST, and 00:00 on July 9 in CST, JST, and KST.

items.label: Steam release examples
items.detail: Examples include 15:00 PDT on July 9, 18:00 EDT on July 9, 23:00 BST on July 9, 06:00 CST on July 10, and 07:00 JST/KST on July 10.

items.label: PS5 preload
items.detail: PS preload starts July 7 at 00:00 local in JP/Asia and July 8 at 00:00 local in EU/US.

items.label: Xbox and Steam preload
items.detail: Xbox preload starts July 7 at 9:00 JST / 2:00 CEST / July 6 at 17:00 PDT. Steam preload is not available.

echoesBeginnerGuide.title: Echoes of Aincrad Beginner Guide: First Hours Checklist
echoesBeginnerGuide.intro: Echoes of Aincrad starts with survival, exploration, and real-time combat inside the floating castle. Your early progress comes from understanding weapons, Sword Skills, Growth Points, Cardinal Rank, partners, items, and dungeon preparation.

steps.title: Create your avatar and enter Aincrad
steps.description: Start by setting up your player character with customization options such as hairstyle, skin tone, and body type. The game is built around playing as your own hero rather than only controlling established Sword Art Online characters.
steps.tip: Treat your avatar as your long-term build base because gear, stats, weapons, and partner synergy all grow around your character.

steps.title: Use weapons as your class
steps.description: There are six weapon types: Sword and Shield, Two-Handed Axe, Dagger, Rapier, Two-Handed Sword, and Mace. Using a weapon earns Weapon Exp, unlocks Sword Skills, and lets you equip up to three Sword Skills at a time.
steps.tip: Start with Sword and Shield for the safest learning curve, then test faster or heavier weapons between quests.

steps.title: Spend Growth Points on the right stats
steps.description: Leveling up gives Growth Points that can be used in your room at the Inn. Strength, Dexterity, Agility, and Intelligence affect weapon-skill damage, while Vitality, Endurance, and Mind improve Health Points, Stamina Points, and SP-related effectiveness.
steps.tip: Raise the stats that match your equipped weapon instead of spreading points randomly.

steps.title: Raise Cardinal Rank carefully
steps.description: Cardinal Rank shows your overall proficiency and affects what you can buy from merchants or forge at the Smithy. It also raises monster difficulty to a degree, improves monster drops, and increases partner abilities.
steps.tip: Whenever Cardinal Rank rises, upgrade weapons and spend Growth Points before taking on the next quest.

steps.title: Explore maps, checkpoints, and dungeons
steps.description: Each new quest or dungeon requires exploration to reveal the map. Checkpoints expose nearby points of interest, including treasure chests, hidden routes, sealed shortcuts, and special mechanisms.
steps.tip: Activate checkpoints early so you can find treasures and shortcuts before committing to dangerous fights.

steps.title: Prepare items before leaving town
steps.description: The Town of Beginnings includes merchants and crafting options. You can buy items with Col, craft healing potions, use materials from the field, and prepare special tools such as mines for combat or environmental interactions.
steps.tip: Stock healing items before long quests because boss fights can lock you in once they begin.

steps.title: Choose a partner for each quest
steps.description: Partners join you during quests and bring their own fighting style, Support Skills, and Combination Skills. Partners can heal, buff, hinder enemies, or join you in powerful combo attacks.
steps.tip: Use Switch Mode when you need breathing room and Free Mode when you want to focus enemies down together.

steps.title: Upgrade and customize gear at the Smithy
steps.description: The Smithy can craft weapons and armor from recipes, upgrade weapon parameters, and combine same-type weapons to build EX-MOD effects. EX-MODs can provide advantages such as reduced Stamina cost or improved normal attack damage.
steps.tip: Keep improving your main weapon type, but save useful EX-MODs for future builds.

echoesWeaponsTierList.title: Echoes of Aincrad Best Weapons Tier List
echoesWeaponsTierList.intro: Weapons act like classes in Echoes of Aincrad. Each type has a different attack style, Sword Skill feel, shield access, and risk level, so the best weapon depends on whether you want safety, speed, stagger, or heavy damage.

tiers.summary: Best beginner safety and most forgiving first choice.

weapons.name: Sword and Shield
weapons.reason: This is the tutorial starting weapon and the safest early pick because it gives both offensive and defensive options. Players can keep the shield equipped for protection or remove it for faster, riskier movement.

tiers.summary: Strong choices with clear combat roles and good long-term value.

weapons.name: Rapier
weapons.reason: Rapier is a light weapon with long reach and a flowing style. It can chain attacks, counter enemies, and still be used with a shield, making it strong for players who want speed without giving up defense.

weapons.name: Mace
weapons.damageStyle: Heavy hits, defense breaking, stagger, and knockdown
weapons.reason: Mace is valuable against harder enemies because it specializes in breaking defenses, staggering targets, and knocking enemies down. Shield access also gives it better safety than most heavy options.

tiers.summary: Powerful but less forgiving; better after learning enemy timing.

weapons.name: Dagger
weapons.damageStyle: Fast hits, short range, lower average damage, throwing options through certain Sword Skills
weapons.reason: Dagger is light and fast, but its shorter range and no-shield setup make positioning more important. It works well for players who like speed and constant pressure.

weapons.name: Two-Handed Sword
weapons.reason: Two-Handed Sword can charge attacks for greater damage, but it cannot use a shield and changes dodge movement into a roll. It rewards timing but is less forgiving for new players.

weapons.name: Two-Handed Axe
weapons.damageStyle: Slower attacks, long reach, and area-of-effect pressure
weapons.reason: Two-Handed Axe is built for powerful offense and area attacks, but mistimed swings can leave the player open. It is better after learning enemy reach, boss windows, and Stamina control.

echoesCombatAndBossGuide.title: Echoes of Aincrad Combat and Boss Guide
echoesCombatAndBossGuide.intro: Combat in Echoes of Aincrad is built around resource management, weapon choice, partner coordination, and preparation before dangerous encounters. Boss battles are especially important because once a boss fight begins, you cannot retreat.

faqs.question: How do Stamina and SP work in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Attacking, dodging, and guarding with a shield consume Stamina. Sword Skills unleash powerful attacks but rapidly drain SP, so using them at the wrong time can leave you vulnerable.
highlights: Running out limits your options in combat

faqs.question: Which defensive tools should beginners use in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Beginners should learn dodging, guarding, and parries before relying on damage alone. Sword and Shield is the safest learning weapon because it gives a balanced mix of attack and defense.
highlights: Use parries when enemy timing becomes clear

faqs.question: How should Sword Skills be used in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Sword Skills are strong attacks tied to your weapon type, but they drain SP quickly. Equip up to three Sword Skills and use them when enemies are open rather than spamming them at the start of every fight.
highlights: Up to three Sword Skills can be equipped

faqs.question: How do healing and support items work in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Healing Crystals restore health and can be replenished at Safety Areas. Healing potions and other consumables can also support longer fights, while mines, explosive stones, and debuff items can create openings when timed well.
highlights: Use mines and debuffs for difficult enemies

faqs.question: How do partners help in Echoes of Aincrad combat?
faqs.answer: Each quest lets you choose a partner with distinct Support Skills and Combination Skills. Switch Mode splits a hard enemy's attention between you and your partner, while Free Mode helps focus down one target or clear multiple enemies one by one.
highlights: Support Skills can heal, buff, or weaken enemies
Combination Skills are powerful joint attacks
Switch Mode and Free Mode can be changed in real time

faqs.question: What should you do before entering a boss room in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Return to base before major boss fights, restock items, upgrade gear, spend Growth Points, and choose the right partner. Once a boss battle begins, it does not end until either you or the boss is defeated.
highlights: Use parries, dodges, and Reversal Slash

faqs.question: Why is map exploration important before combat in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Activating checkpoints reveals the surrounding map and marks useful points of interest such as treasure chests, special mechanisms, hidden routes, and shortcuts. Better map knowledge reduces wasted fights and helps you prepare before dangerous areas.
highlights: Treasure chests can contain useful rewards
Shortcuts and hidden paths support safer progression

faqs.question: How do you keep your build ready for harder enemies in Echoes of Aincrad?
faqs.answer: Use Growth Points to strengthen stats, buy or craft items with Col and gathered materials, and upgrade, fuse, or craft weapons at the blacksmith. EX-MODs can add special effects and help shape your preferred combat style.

echoesCharacterAndPartnerGuide.title: Echoes of Aincrad Character Creation and Partner Guide
echoesCharacterAndPartnerGuide.intro: Echoes of Aincrad lets players enter Sword Art Online as an original customizable protagonist. Partners are not just story companions: they bring different weapons, support options, combo attacks, and tactical behavior that directly affect survival in quests and boss preparation.

cards.title: Customizable Protagonist
cards.description: Players create their own hero instead of playing only as an existing SAO character. Character creation opens at the end of the Sword Art Online Beta section and includes pre-made templates plus sliders for detailed avatar customization.
cards.bestFor: Players who want a personal self-insert SAO adventure.

cards.title: Partner Selection
cards.description: At the start of each quest, players choose one partner character to fight alongside them. Partner weapons and skills can be adjusted from the player's room in an Inn located in town.
cards.bestFor: Preparing a quest setup before entering dungeons or major encounters.

cards.title: Iori
cards.description: Iori is a sword-wielding solo player who taught the protagonist the basics of Sword Art Online during the beta test.

cards.title: Wyzeman
cards.description: Wyzeman has been a party member since the beta test and is described as a lore enthusiast eager to clear the Sword Art Online death game.
cards.bestFor: Players who want shield-based mace support.

cards.title: Zash
cards.description: Zash is a two-handed sword user who cares for his allies and meets the party while they are looking for a new raid member.
cards.bestFor: Players who want heavy two-handed sword pressure.

cards.title: Argo
cards.description: Argo is a versatile info broker with a sharp eye for money. She met the protagonist during the beta test and fights with a dagger.

cards.title: Support Skills
cards.description: Partners can use Support Skills when called from the action wheel. These skills can include healing, defensive buffs, offensive buffs, and other support effects, and they can be previewed before starting a quest.
cards.bestFor: Staying alive during longer quests and difficult fights.

cards.title: Combination Skills
cards.description: Combination Skills are powerful attacks performed together with a partner after enough damage has been dealt to enemies. Most partners have one or two Combination Skills that can be assigned to the action wheel.
cards.bestFor: Turning the tide during hard enemy encounters.

cards.title: Switch Mode
cards.description: Switch Mode lets the partner draw enemy focus, giving the player breathing room for timed attacks or safer repositioning.
cards.bestFor: Managing dangerous single targets and reducing pressure.

cards.title: Free Mode
cards.description: Free Mode helps the player and partner focus down one enemy together or clear multiple enemies one by one.
cards.bestFor: Finishing targets quickly or cleaning up groups efficiently.

echoesDeathGameModeGuide.title: Echoes of Aincrad Death Game Mode Guide
echoesDeathGameModeGuide.intro: Death Game Mode changes the meaning of every fight. It can be played at any difficulty, but if the player dies in this mode, that character save is erased and cannot be reused, so weapon choice, partner setup, and tactical commands matter much more.

steps.title: Understand the Core Rule
steps.description: Death Game Mode adds permanent consequences to defeat. If the adventure ends in this mode, the character save data is erased.
steps.tip: Treat every dungeon, stat choice, weapon selection, and partner command as part of survival planning.

steps.title: Know What Does Not Delete Data
steps.description: Save data is safe when exiting the game normally, intentionally returning to the title screen, or experiencing unexpected interruptions such as power loss.
steps.tip: The mode is built around in-game defeat, not punishing normal exits or technical interruptions.

steps.title: Use Partner Tactics Carefully
steps.description: The partner stays by the player's side, but poor partner skills, weapon setup, or tactical mode management can leave the player exposed when the fight becomes dangerous.
steps.tip: Pair Support Skills, Combination Skills, Switch Mode, and Free Mode with the encounter type.

steps.title: Unlock It After Clearing the Game
steps.description: For most players, Death Game Mode becomes available after completing the game's story mode.
steps.tip: This path is suited for players who want to learn normal systems before starting a one-life run.

steps.title: Start Early with Deluxe or Ultimate Edition
steps.description: Deluxe Edition and Ultimate Edition include Death Game Mode Early Unlock, allowing players to begin in Death Game Mode from the first playthrough.
steps.tip: Choose this route only when you want the high-risk version from day one.

steps.title: Separate It from Demo Save Transfer
steps.description: Save data carried over from the demo cannot be switched into Death Game Mode. If Death Game Mode is unlocked, it can be selected when creating a second or later save file.
steps.tip: Plan a fresh save file for Death Game Mode instead of converting demo progress.

echoesDemoAndSaveTransferGuide.title: Echoes of Aincrad Demo and Save Transfer Guide
echoesDemoAndSaveTransferGuide.intro: The Echoes of Aincrad demo gives players access to the beta phase of the story, five full missions, and every weapon type. Demo save data can transfer into the full release when the main game is updated and launched on the same platform.

steps.title: Download the Demo
steps.description: The demo is available on PC via Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.
steps.detail: The PC demo was released through Steam Next Fest, while console demo access covers PS5 and Xbox Series X|S.

steps.title: Play the Beta Phase
steps.description: The demo lets players experience the beta phase of the story with five full missions.
steps.detail: This gives players an early look at Aincrad's environments, quest flow, and combat structure.

steps.title: Test Every Weapon Type
steps.description: Every weapon type can be tried in the demo.
steps.detail: Players can use the demo to compare weapon feel before choosing a main playstyle in the full game.

steps.title: Update the Full Game
steps.description: To transfer demo save data, the full game must be updated to the latest version.
steps.detail: Connect to the network and install the latest update before starting the transfer process.

steps.title: Launch the Full Version
steps.description: Start the full game and confirm the required agreements.
steps.detail: When demo save data is detected, choose Carry over from the prompt.

steps.title: Confirm the Transfer
steps.description: On the transfer confirmation screen, press and hold Confirm. After the transfer succeeds, select Close, then choose Game Start from the title screen.
steps.detail: All demo save data slots will be transferred.

steps.title: Load the Transferred Save
steps.description: After transfer, load the demo save data from the save data selection screen.
steps.detail: Transfer works only between the same platform, and demo save data should not be deleted from the device before transfer.

steps.title: Know the Death Game Limitation
steps.description: Save data carried over from the demo cannot be switched to Death Game Mode.
steps.detail: If Death Game Mode is unlocked, it can be selected when creating a second or later save file.

echoesEditionsAndPreOrderBonus.title: Echoes of Aincrad Editions and Pre Order Bonus
echoesEditionsAndPreOrderBonus.intro: Echoes of Aincrad is sold in Standard, Deluxe, and Ultimate editions. All pre-orders include the Proto-Elucidator Series Weapon Pack, while Deluxe and Ultimate add the Expansion DLC Pass, Starter Pack, and Death Game Mode Early Unlock.

rows.starterPack: Included; 50,000 Col, 10 High Healing Potions, 10 High SP Potions, and 10 Mystic Crystals
Included; 50,000 Col, 10 High Healing Potions, 10 High SP Potions, and 10 Mystic Crystals
rows.bonusContentsApp: Included; Unanswered//butterfly Special Movie, 141-track Digital Soundtrack, and 640-page Digital Artbook

rows.preOrderBonus: Includes six Proto-Elucidator weapon types, a shield, and matching blueprints for crafting additional copies

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Echoes of Aincrad

questions.question: What is Echoes of Aincrad?
questions.answer: Echoes of Aincrad is a single-player SAO action JRPG developed by Bandai Namco and Game Studio Inc., launching July 10, 2026 on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam.

questions.question: Is there an Echoes of Aincrad demo?
questions.answer: Yes. The Echoes of Aincrad demo covers the beta phase of the story with five full missions and every weapon type, available on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S.

questions.question: Does Echoes of Aincrad demo save data carry over?
questions.answer: Yes. Demo save data in Echoes of Aincrad can transfer into the full game on the same platform after updating and launching the full version, but it cannot be switched into Death Game Mode.

questions.question: What is Death Game Mode in Echoes of Aincrad?
questions.answer: Death Game Mode is the optional high-stakes mode in Echoes of Aincrad where defeat erases the character save. Deluxe and Ultimate Edition owners can unlock it early for a one-life run from the first playthrough.

questions.question: Which weapons are in Echoes of Aincrad?
questions.answer: Echoes of Aincrad has six weapon types: Sword and Shield, Two-Handed Axe, Dagger, Rapier, Two-Handed Sword, and Mace. Sword and Shield is the safest beginner pick.

questions.question: Is Echoes of Aincrad on Steam Deck or Switch?
questions.answer: Echoes of Aincrad is not officially listed for Nintendo Switch. Steam Deck support is not confirmed by the official specs, so players should check the Steam store page before buying.

This section covers the official release date, regional unlock times, available editions, and preload windows so you know exactly when and where to start playing.
